objective: Reconcile whether TransitionRecovery is a required RootTaxonomyV3 release-gate root or optional overlay evidence before any completion claim.

why_transition_recovery_is_optional_now: 010718 schema defines TransitionRecovery as optional root until consumer contract decides.
010718 crosswalk sets TransitionRecovery contract_state to optional root until downstream consumer explicitly requires it.
No downstream consumer contract in the current Board A evidence requires TransitionRecovery for trade usability or release-gate coverage.
contract_decision.consumer_activation_rule: If a downstream consumer later requires TransitionRecovery, treat it as an active required root and rerun or explicitly carry forward the accepted 004210 packet under the then-current schema.
contract_decision.completion_rule: For this superseded RootTaxonomyV3 branch, completion would have required every required release-gate root to have calibrated 95% evidence and cross-market/timeframe validation; optional TransitionRecovery evidence cannot compensate for missing required roots. The active board cursor now uses MainRegimeV2 instead.

transition_recovery_004210.interpretation: Useful optional/overlay packet, not required release-gate coverage under the current Board A contract.

transition_recovery_003739.interpretation: Broader base rule failed; do not use it as acceptance.

schema_input_010718.transition_recovery_contract: optional root until downstream consumer explicitly requires it
schema_input_010718.decision_note: 010718 listed TransitionRecovery in the wider missing evidence inventory; this audit separates optional inventory state from required release-gate coverage.

gate_state_after_audit.blocker: required roots missing 95 evidence: BullExpansion, BearExpansion, Consolidation, Manipulation
gate_state_after_audit.next_action: Acquire higher-signal signed-direction/range inputs and aligned historical manipulation L2/L3/order-lifecycle data, then rerun unchanged RootTaxonomyV3 gates for the missing required roots.
